title: Varnish Worker Thread Queue Backlog
spl:
index=cache sourcetype="varnish:stats"
| timechart span=1m max(n_wrk_queue) as worker_queue_depth by host
| where worker_queue_depth > 50
description: `n_wrk_queue` grows when worker threads cannot dequeue requests fast enough. Backlog leads to elevated latency and client timeouts.
value: Detects thread starvation before Varnish stops keeping up with traffic spikes.
implementation: Poll `varnishstat -j` every 30‚Äď60s; flatten `MAIN.n_wrk_queue` to `n_wrk_queue`. Correlate with `cache_hit` drops and backend latency.
visualization: Line chart (queue depth), overlay with `n_wrk_busy`, table (peaks by POP).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom scripted input (`varnishstat -j`).
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cache` `sourcetype=varnish:stats` (`MAIN.n_wrk_queue` / `n_wrk_queue`).
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
Ensure JSON stats include worker queue; confirm field name matches your extractor (`n_wrk_queue`).

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cache sourcetype="varnish:stats"
| timechart span=1m max(n_wrk_queue) as worker_queue_depth by host
| where worker_queue_depth > 50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Varnish Worker Thread Queue Backlog** ‚ÄĒ See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cache` `sourcetype=varnish:stats` (`MAIN.n_wrk_queue` / `n_wrk_queue`). **App/TA**: Custom scripted input (`varnishstat -j`). Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Line chart (queue depth), overlay with `n_wrk_busy`, table (peaks by POP)..
grandmaExplanation: We watch the line of work waiting inside your cache so you can add capacity before pages time out.
